title: System for fastening strings of a stringed instrument
abstract: A system for fastening strings of a stringed instrument is disclosed. The system incorporates a bridge unit (100) and a nut unit (200) to engage two ends of strings of a stringed instrument. The bridge (100) is mounted on a body of the instrument and can rotate by the help of a lever (112) coupled to the bridge unit. The bridge (100) incorporates a locking means to lock the bridge (100) at a pre-defined angle. The bridge incorporates a plurality of string anchor means (106) to engage one end of the strings. The nut unit (200) configured to limit the deflection of second end of the strings. The plurality of string anchor means (106) includes height adjustment means (132) and string intonation means (136) to adjust height and intonation of the strings respectively.
claims: Claims We Claim: 1. A system for fastening strings of a stringed instrument, the system comprising: a bridge unit pivotally coupled to a body of the stringed instrument, said bridge unit comprising a plurality of string anchor means for engaging one end of a plurality of strings of the stringed instrument, wherein the bridge unit pivots about a first axis extending transversely to the strings of the stringed instrument and parallel to the top surface of the stringed instrument body and enables movement of the one end of the plurality of strings in the direction of the plurality of strings; a nut unit configured to a neck of the stringed instrument and at a distal end defined at a position away from the body of the stringed instrument, said nut unit comprising: a plurality of grooves for engaging a second end of the plurality of strings of the stringed instrument; and a plurality of clamping means configured to limit deflection of the second end of the plurality of strings at the plurality of grooves; a lever coupled to said bridge unit and configured to enable rotation of the bridge unit; and a locking means configured with the bridge unit and having the lever coupled to it, wherein rotation of the lever enables the locking means to constrain the bridge unit at a pre-defined angle about the first axis. 2. The system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the bridge unit comprises one or more springs configured to counter a moment produced due to movement of the one end of the plurality of strings in the direction of the plurality of strings. 3. The system as claimed in claim 2, wherein a spring compression adjuster is configured with the one or more springs to adjust its compression. 4. The system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein an intonation adjustment means is configured to the plurality of string anchor means to adjust intonation of the plurality of strings of the stringed instrument. 5. The system as claimed in claim 1, wherein a height adjustment means is configured to the plurality of string anchor means to adjust the height of the plurality of string anchor means relative to the bridge unit. 6. The system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the bridge unit is pivotally coupled to the body of the stringed instrument by a shaft and bearing means. 7. The system as claimed in claim 1, wherein the locking means comprises a locking shaft with a wedge profile, wherein the locking shaft is configured to rotate in a hole positioned in the bridge unit. 8. The system as claimed in claim 7, wherein the bridge unit comprises a channel for engagement of the wedge profile of the locking shaft, when the bridge unit is in locked position. 9. The system as claimed in claim 7, wherein the lever is configured to facilitate rotation of the locking shaft in the hole of the bridge unit. 10. The system as claimed in claim 1, wherein surface of the plurality of grooves and surface of the plurality of clamping means of the nut unit are having a curvature, which enables limiting the deflection of the second end of the plurality of strings at the nut unit.
